source: CIS AWS DATABASE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1
title: Ensure Monitor and Audit Activity is enabled

input_row.source: CIS AWS DATABASE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1
input_row.title: Ensure Monitor and Audit Activity is enabled
input_row.description: Regular monitoring and auditing of activity in Amazon DynamoDB help ensure your database's security, performance, and compliance.
input_row.rationale: This keeps track and ensures who has recently modified a document and monitors all activity within the database. This information allows the individual to use the details provided for auditing purposes and to address any compliance requirements.
input_row.audit: 1. Enable CloudTrail Logging for DynamoDB • Sign in to the AWS Management Console and open the CloudTrail console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/cloudtrail/. • Choose Trails from the left-side menu. • Click Create trail or select an existing trail. • Specify a trail name, choose an S3 bucket for storing logs, and configure other trail settings. • Under Data events, select the checkbox for DynamoDB to enable logging of DynamoDB data events. • Click Create trail or Save changes to save the CloudTrail configuration. 2. Enable DynamoDB Streams • Sign in to the AWS Management Console and open the DynamoDB console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/dynamodb/. • Select the DynamoDB table you want to monitor. • Click on the Overview tab. • Under the DynamoDB Streams section, click Manage stream. • Enable DynamoDB Streams with the desired view type (e.g., New and old images). • Click Enable. 3. Configure Amazon CloudWatch Alarms • Sign in to the AWS Management Console and open the CloudWatch console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/cloudwatch/. • In the left-side menu, click on Alarms.  • Click Create alarm. • Select a DynamoDB metric to monitor (e.g., Read or Write capacity units). • Configure the threshold, conditions, and actions for the alarm. • Choose the actions to take when the alarm state is triggered (e.g., send notifications, auto-scaling actions, etc.). • Click Create alarm to save the configuration. 4. Analyze and Review Logs and Metrics • Sign in to the AWS Management Console and open the CloudWatch console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/cloudwatch/. • In the left-side menu, click Logs to access CloudWatch Logs. • Select the appropriate log group for DynamoDB (e.g., /aws/dynamodb/TableName). • Review the logs to monitor activities, errors, and any unusual behavior. • Navigate to the CloudWatch console and click Metrics in the left-side menu. • Select the DynamoDB namespace and the desired metrics (e.g., ConsumedReadCapacityUnits, ConsumedWriteCapacityUnits). • Analyze the metrics to identify trends, capacity needs, and potential issues. 5. Enable AWS Config for DynamoDB • Sign in to the AWS Management Console and open the AWS Config console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/config/. • Click on Rules in the left-side menu. • Click Add rule. • Configure a rule for DynamoDB compliance checks, such as checking for unencrypted tables or insecure IAM policies. • Customize the rule settings and scope based on your requirements. • Click Save to create the AWS Config rule.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**

The control to ensure monitoring and auditing activity in Amazon DynamoDB can be automated due to the following reasons:

✓ The audit process involves configurations of services like CloudTrail, DynamoDB Streams, CloudWatch Alarms, and AWS Config, all of which can be queried and configured via AWS APIs and CLI.

✓ CloudTrail logging, DynamoDB Streams, and CloudWatch monitoring are standard AWS services that expose their configurations via APIs.

✓ The aws_auditing_tools such as boto3 SDK have specific methods to check these configurations:
  - CloudTrail can be checked using `boto3.client('cloudtrail').describe_trails()` to verify trails and `get_event_selectors` to check data events for DynamoDB.
  - DynamoDB Streams can be accessed with `boto3.client('dynamodbstreams').list_streams()` and `describe_stream` for configuration.
  - CloudWatch alarms and metrics can be verified with `boto3.client('cloudwatch').describe_alarms()` and `list_metrics()`.
  - AWS Config rules can be checked with `boto3.client('config').describe_config_rules()`.

✓ No component of the audit requires human judgment or an environment outside of AWS services.

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_dynamodb_monitoring_audit_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**

- **CloudTrail Configuration:**
  - Use `boto3.client('cloudtrail').describe_trails()` to verify that a trail exists.
  - Use `boto3.client('cloudtrail').get_event_selectors()` to ensure that DynamoDB data events are selected for logging.

- **DynamoDB Streams:**
  - Use `boto3.client('dynamodbstreams').list_streams()` to ensure that streams are enabled for the desired table.
  - Verify specific stream settings using `boto3.client('dynamodbstreams').describe_stream()`.

- **CloudWatch Alarms:**
  - Validate the existence and configuration of alarms using `boto3.client('cloudwatch').describe_alarms()`.
  - Ensure that alarms monitor appropriate DynamoDB metrics, like Read/Write capacity, and check their thresholds and actions.

- **AWS Config Rules:**
  - List and describe AWS Config rules using `boto3.client('config').describe_config_rules()`.
  - Ensure there are rules set for DynamoDB compliance checks, reviewing rule scope and settings for encryption and IAM policy security.

- **Pass/Fail Criteria:**
  - Ensure all the required monitoring configurations are enabled and properly set, with no misconfigurations to ensure full compliance.

The automation across these aspects confirms that monitoring and auditing in DynamoDB accounts is correctly configured according to the CIS Benchmark.
